architecture.summary: This is a survey paper organizing the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) paradigm into four sequential stages: pre-retrieval (query preparation), retrieval (information extraction from external sources), post-retrieval (result processing), and generation (LLM output production). RAG integrates information retrieval techniques with deep learning to inject up-to-date external information into LLMs at generation time, addressing the static knowledge and hallucination problems of base LLMs.

biblio.title: A Survey on Retrieval-Augmented Text Generation for Large Language Models

claims.statement: RAG merges information retrieval (IR) techniques with deep learning advancements to address the static limitations of large language models (LLMs) by enabling the dynamic integration of up-to-date external information

RAG provides a cost-effective solution to the generation of plausible but possibly incorrect responses by LLMs, thereby enhancing the accuracy and reliability of their outputs through the use of real-world data
The RAG paradigm is organized into four categories: pre-retrieval, retrieval, post-retrieval, and generation, offering a detailed perspective from the retrieval viewpoint
